title: Marge Vs Singles Seniors Childless Coupl

jokes.text: D'oh! (SCREAMING) (BRAKES SCREECHING)
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation after crashing
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase perfectly integrated into character moment. Loses originality points for being established running gag.

jokes.text: Oh, crikey! She's got me by the dangle-down!
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic term 'dangle-down' for male genitalia
jokes.explanation: Mildly clever euphemism that gets laugh from unexpected childish terminology in dangerous situation.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm watching that! / Not anymore.
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact dismissal
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline showcasing marital dynamic. Marge's dry delivery is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: We'll also show you how to turn a shoebox into a you-box.
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical 'you-box' wordplay
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that doesn't quite land. The absurdity of dollhouse home improvement is funnier than the pun itself.

jokes.text: Oh, I missed the feeding frenzy. I hope you're happy. / Obviously, yes.
jokes.setup: Homer sarcastically asks if Marge is happy about his missed TV
jokes.punchline: Marge's blunt confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Marge's unexpected directness subverts Homer's expectation of sympathy.

jokes.text: Bobby, I got propane in my urethra. / We'll be right back with Who Will Marry a Million Bears!
jokes.punchline: Cutting to absurd reality show title
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurd TV parody. The juxtaposition of medical emergency with ridiculous reality show is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Mom, that's Roofi. His music is why babies are idiots.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's intellectual disdain for children's entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa line - her precocious intelligence applied to dismissing children's entertainment. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Well, Maggie likes Roofi. And babies only like good things.
jokes.punchline: Marge's flawed logic about baby preferences
jokes.explanation: Marge's maternal defensiveness leads to absurd logical conclusion. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: (IN PIG LATIN) Ap-cray. Ap-cray.
jokes.punchline: Bart calling the music 'crap' in pig Latin
jokes.explanation: Clever way for Bart to curse without getting in trouble. Fits his mischievous character perfectly.

jokes.text: it's on batteries. / Backed up by solar power.
jokes.setup: Roofi CD keeps playing despite attempts to stop it
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it has backup power source
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's frustration with unstoppable children's music. The solar power detail adds absurd persistence.

jokes.text: Look, Maggie. Funny Daddy! (GROANING)
jokes.setup: Homer driven to breakdown by repetitive children's song
jokes.punchline: Marge treating Homer's mental breakdown as entertainment for baby
jokes.explanation: Dark parenting humor - treating spouse's suffering as baby entertainment

jokes.text: You don't understand. I can't go home. You got to give me detention. Look! I fed the gerbil coffee!
jokes.setup: Bart desperately wants to stay at school
jokes.punchline: Deliberately committing pet abuse to get detention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - Bart trying to get IN trouble. The specific act of feeding coffee to a gerbil is perfectly Bart-like mischief.

jokes.text: Please, make me write something on the chalkboard a thousand times! / We all got tired of that chalkboard years ago.
jokes.setup: Bart begging for classic Simpsons punishment
jokes.punchline: Skinner's meta comment about the show's own trope
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about the show's own opening sequence trope. Self-aware without being smug, and perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Her eyes aren't focused. / it makes her happy. / So does sucking on the dog.
jokes.setup: Discussion about what makes baby Maggie happy
jokes.punchline: Homer's disturbing comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer gross-out humor that undermines Marge's maternal defense. Gets laugh through shock value.

jokes.text: If you don't come, Roofi will be sad. And the helpful bear, she will die!
jokes.punchline: Threatening character death to manipulate children
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on children's entertainment marketing. The casual threat of character death is absurdly manipulative.

jokes.text: Give Daddy the remote, Maggie. Give Daddy the... D'oh! / Oh, wise guy, eh?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get remote from baby
jokes.punchline: Baby outsmarts Homer, causing his signature frustration
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer vs. Maggie dynamic. The 'wise guy' response shows Homer treating baby as equal adversary.

jokes.text: I got them fair and square and I kept my dignity, not like Janie's mom. (nose twisting noise) / Whatever.
jokes.setup: Marge proud of getting tickets honorably
jokes.punchline: Other mom's Three Stooges-style injury and dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of parent desperation at children's event. The casual 'whatever' after injury adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: This concert is oversold. It's as if a music promoter acted unscrupulously.
jokes.punchline: Homer's faux-naive observation about obvious corruption
jokes.explanation: Homer's mock surprise at obvious scam. The formal language 'unscrupulously' contrasts with his usual vocabulary.

jokes.text: And now, our opening act, in their first live show since Tinky Winky was acquitted of manslaughter. / Not guilty!
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to serious criminal charges
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy juxtaposing innocent children's characters with serious criminal charges. Tinky Winky's proud response is perfect.

jokes.text: They make The Blue Man Group look like Mummenschanz, which is still pretty good.
jokes.punchline: Obscure performance art comparison with qualification
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated reference humor showing Homer's unexpected cultural knowledge. The qualifying 'still pretty good' is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: Oh, no! It's raining! And Maggie's diapers are extra absorbent.
jokes.punchline: Worry about baby's diaper absorption capacity
jokes.explanation: Clever parenting observation - turning diaper feature into liability. Very Marge-like practical concern.

jokes.text: You kidding me? Did you make funny faces? You did? Well, did you jiggle your keys? I did it all, man!
jokes.punchline: Desperate listing of basic child entertainment techniques
jokes.explanation: Good portrayal of children's entertainer desperation. The progression from simple to basic shows complete arsenal exhausted.

jokes.text: Ow! Okay, who threw that? Who threw it?
jokes.punchline: Getting pelted with objects mid-song
jokes.explanation: Standard performer-gets-heckled gag. Works for the riot buildup but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Tyler, is there a peaceful solution possible? (TYLER COOING)
jokes.punchline: Baby can only make baby noises, not negotiate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise - treating baby riot as serious political situation. The cooing response is perfect anticlimax.

jokes.text: Five, six, so long, hicks!
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to insult audience and flee
jokes.explanation: Perfect breaking point for children's entertainer. Using his own song structure to deliver the insult is clever.

jokes.text: I don't feel right clubbing women and children, chief. / I hear ya. Some days are tougher than others. Just close your eyes and club.
jokes.punchline: Chief's casual advice about violence against civilians
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy perfect for Chief Wiggum's incompetent authority figure. The casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: This is it, boys. We'll have to tummy-kiss our way out. (BLOWING TUMMY KISSES)
jokes.punchline: Military-style tactical use of baby entertainment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation treating baby entertainment as military tactic. The serious tone with silly action is perfect.

jokes.text: I ain't fungified hidee-hoo about no legrification noways, then scratched his rear, hitched up his pants, and scratched his rear again.
jokes.setup: News report on farmer's response to legal action
jokes.punchline: Mangled hillbilly speech and redundant rear-scratching
jokes.explanation: Standard Cletus hillbilly characterization. The made-up words and behavioral details fit character but aren't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Kabul refuses to be our sister city anymore. I have no sister city!
jokes.setup: Mayor listing consequences of baby riot
jokes.punchline: International diplomatic fallout over baby behavior, personal anguish
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of consequences, absurd international implications of local baby riot

jokes.text: My loose change!
jokes.setup: Politician announces money will come from taxpayers
jokes.punchline: Immediate concern about small change being taken
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about taxpayer anxiety. Gets mild laugh but nothing special.

jokes.text: Singles Seniors Childless Couples and Teens and Gays against Parasitic Parents. / Catchy name.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about its catchiness
jokes.explanation: Good satirical jab at unwieldy organization names. The deadpan response lands the irony well.

jokes.text: Save your breath for blowing up water wings, breeder.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to speak at anti-child rally
jokes.punchline: Dismissive insult with specific parenting reference
jokes.explanation: Sharp, cruel put-down with specific imagery. 'Breeder' as insult and the water wings detail show creative hostility.

jokes.text: I dream of an America with nudity and F-words on network TV! Where the whole world doesn't stop because a school bus did.
jokes.punchline: Specific complaints about child-centric society
jokes.explanation: Good satirical take on adult frustrations with child-friendly society. The school bus detail is particularly relatable.

jokes.text: We never would've had these kids if we thought we had to pay for them! Promises were made! / Tough tortellini!
jokes.punchline: Food-based dismissive phrase instead of standard expression
jokes.explanation: Mildly clever food-based variation on 'tough luck.' Gets laugh from unexpected word choice.

jokes.text: I'm sick of printing a children's menu! Let Mickey Meatball find his own way out of the maze!
jokes.punchline: Specific children's menu character reference
jokes.explanation: Creative specific complaint with made-up character name. The maze reference adds to the absurdity of the grievance.

jokes.text: Then how would we ever get to see Camelot? / We'll merely watch the movie on tape! / Is that better? / To me, Ralph Wiggum is Sir Lancelot.
jokes.setup: Debate about school plays vs movies
jokes.punchline: Parent's delusional pride in Ralph's acting
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of Ralph's established character. Parent's blind devotion to clearly terrible performance is very relatable.

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, let's kill every child... ...friendly thing in town!
jokes.setup: Dramatic pause suggesting violence against children
jokes.punchline: Completing sentence to mean child-friendly businesses
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke with perfect timing. The pause creates genuine shock before revealing the harmless intent.

jokes.text: (SQUEAKY VOICE) it's time to put away childish things (DEEP VOICE) and become a man!
jokes.punchline: Voice cracking during declaration of maturity
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic undermining of adult proclamation. Comic Book Guy's adolescent voice crack during maturity speech is ideal.

jokes.text: From now on, children acting up in public places will be lightly tased. / Mommy, pick me up. (ELECTRICITY CRACKLING) / I wasn't kidding.
jokes.setup: News about new child discipline policy
jokes.punchline: Immediate demonstration on innocent child request
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation. Kent's matter-of-fact delivery and immediate demonstration of extreme policy is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: And I wouldn't trade her for all the sleep-in Sundays and speed dating in the world.
jokes.punchline: Specific sacrifices parents make listed as worthless compared to children
jokes.explanation: Sweet maternal sentiment with specific details about single life. Works for character but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Well, Lisa, I would be proud if one of the eggs I sold turned out like you.
jokes.punchline: Revealing she sells her eggs, treating reproduction as business
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment revealing Lindsay's commodified view of reproduction. Perfectly fits her business-focused personality.

jokes.text: Also Maggie puked in your purse again. / Poor me. All my purse is full of is disposable income.
jokes.setup: Child creating mess in mother's purse
jokes.punchline: Childless woman's sarcastic complaint about having money instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between parent and childless person's problems. Lindsay's mock sympathy is well-delivered.

jokes.text: Mom, can we get a pretzel? / We've got pretzels at home. / Not cinnamon.
jokes.setup: Child asking for treat, mother giving standard response
jokes.punchline: Child's specific counterargument about variety
jokes.explanation: Relatable parent-child exchange. The specific 'not cinnamon' detail shows child's superior knowledge of available options.

jokes.text: PPASSCCATAG is also a disease of the brainstem. That's how I'll remember it.
jokes.punchline: Doctor's medical association with the sound
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific joke. Dr. Hibbert's medical perspective on the acronym sound is appropriately professional.

jokes.text: The only petitions that I sign are to bring back canceled sitcoms. Thank you. America needs the wisdom of Herman's Head now more than ever.
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to obscure 90s sitcom as essential
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference joke. Herman's Head being called essential wisdom is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Now we own you! / But I haven't endorsed it yet! / Football injury.
jokes.setup: Tobacco lobby claiming ownership of politician
jokes.punchline: Dismissing objection as brain injury
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of political corruption. The casual dismissal as brain injury is appropriately callous.

jokes.text: Yes. Particularly their supple young organs.
jokes.setup: Burns claiming to care about children
jokes.punchline: Revealing sinister medical interest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mr. Burns line - appearing charitable while revealing evil intent. The specific 'supple young organs' is deliciously villainous.

jokes.text: Oh, unfenced backyard pools, where would I be without you?
jokes.punchline: Expressing gratitude for dangerous pool situations
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Burns moment - finding joy in child endangerment. The poetic phrasing makes his evil more amusing.

jokes.text: (IMITATING MARGE) I'm Marge Simpson, and even I'm against 'Families Come First.' Now it's time to do some coke off the blade of a knife.
jokes.punchline: Immediately switching to drug use after political statement
jokes.explanation: Great satire of deceptive political ads. The immediate shift to drug use is perfectly absurd contrast to family values.

jokes.text: Now it's time to do some coke off the blade of a knife.
jokes.setup: Anti-family political ad using fake Marge actress
jokes.punchline: Actress breaking character with extremely inappropriate drug use activity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of fake political ad to absurd extreme behavior

jokes.text: Maybe she was you and you're not!
jokes.setup: Marge denying she appeared in attack ad
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused identity philosophy
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logical confusion. His attempt at philosophical depth results in nonsense, perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And when you can't finish a sandwich, I eat that sandwich.
jokes.punchline: Including food cleanup as marital duty
jokes.explanation: Sweet Homer moment ruined by his food obsession. Mixing romantic sentiment with sandwich cleanup is very Homer.

jokes.text: I'll come down on those guys like the garage door on Bart's bike!
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to presumably repeated household accident
jokes.explanation: Good specific Homer threat. The garage door/bike reference suggests this happens often enough to be a reliable comparison.

jokes.text: But newspaper writers are a bunch of jerks. Who really opposes 'Families Come First?' Many childless advocates are like Ben Affleck. Famous, successful people from out of state!
jokes.punchline: Random Ben Affleck comparison as political villain
jokes.explanation: Mediocre political ad parody. The Ben Affleck reference feels random rather than clever.

jokes.text: Many childless advocates are like Ben Affleck. Famous, successful people from out of state!
jokes.punchline: Using Ben Affleck as example of outsider influence with weak connection
jokes.explanation: Weak political ad humor relying on random celebrity name-drop

jokes.text: For more information, visit our Web site, www.al-jazeera.com. We're not affiliated. We're just piggy-backing on their message board.
jokes.punchline: Admitting to hijacking unrelated news site
jokes.explanation: Good absurd tech joke. The casual admission of hijacking Al Jazeera's message board is perfectly Homer-level incompetence.

jokes.text: It says, 'Yes on 232.' We want no on 232! Yes on 242! Either way. The important thing is the system works.
jokes.punchline: Giving up on accuracy in favor of democratic participation
jokes.explanation: Good satire of political campaign incompetence. The final 'system works' conclusion despite total confusion is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm the visionary. You come up with the nuts and bolts. / You don't even know what the idea is. / I know you have an ugly face.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming credit for Lisa's idea
jokes.punchline: Resorting to childish insult when challenged
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling argument escalation. Bart's immediate resort to insults when logic fails is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You don't even know what the idea is. I know you have an ugly face.
jokes.setup: Lisa claiming credit for Bart's idea
jokes.punchline: Bart's childish insult deflecting from his lack of actual plan
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling argument with childish insult, authentic but not special

jokes.text: I love you. / I love glue! / I love you! / Aw! Such a sweet little thing. Time to destroy your future.
jokes.punchline: Adult's sweet reaction followed by malicious political intent
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy juxtaposition. The immediate shift from 'sweet little thing' to 'destroy your future' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: I love you. I love glue! I love you!
jokes.setup: Children being used as political weapons to spread love
jokes.punchline: Child including inappropriate object of affection with family members
jokes.explanation: Decent child logic humor mixing appropriate and inappropriate loves

jokes.text: Feeling flu-ish, fever, nausea, child germs. No. Must participate in democratic process.
jokes.setup: Adult getting sick from contact with children
jokes.punchline: Overcoming illness for civic duty
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about children as disease vectors. The civic duty angle adds some nobility to the gross-out humor.

jokes.text: For all their disposable income, for all their leisure time, they had no immunity against God's lowliest creatures, children.
jokes.punchline: Being defeated by 'God's lowliest creatures'
jokes.explanation: Good War of the Worlds parody structure. Calling children 'God's lowliest creatures' is amusingly dismissive.

jokes.text: Great. Let's dump these kids in an R-rated movie while we go someplace nice. / Can I bring my laser pointer? / What do I care?
jokes.punchline: Complete indifference to Bart's mischief plans
jokes.explanation: Good return to bad parenting status quo. Homer's total indifference to Bart's theater disruption plans is perfectly negligent.

tmdb_overview: When a group of singles, seniors, childless couples, teens and gays band together to protest having to accommodate families (including paying property taxes used to teach children they do not have) after a riot at a children's concert, Marge takes up the cause for the families, with some help from Bart and Lisa.
